feed.title: lqnub’s homework
feed.subtitle: Just trying to make a simple blog

entry.title: This is a test!
entry.content:
What did we learn?

“HTML is just another MD”. I mean yes… but no…

In general, of course HTML is another markup language. I mean, it’s in the name itself.

But in terms of practical use, the existance of elements that have a semantical meaning that has an effect on the structure of the content itself gives it another layer of complexity that you wouldn’t even think about if you were only going to write another Markdown.

Furthermore, given this structure of HTML itself, the ability of structuring code that.

Writing vanilla CSS ain’t pretty

Yeah… just please don’t unless you’re an actual designer and are willing to spend a lot of your time deciding what things are more importants than others.

So what do we do instead of vanilla CSS?

Just use a tool. Most tools are designed by a large group of developers who have gone through the problems that writing vanilla CSS generates. Their solutions address these problems and furthermore, they’re made so that even people without much designing experience can be able to do things quickly that are aesthetically good looking for most peple.

Just take a look at this text box with some C++ that was made with barely any effort. I don’t wanna read the obscure things that are hidden behind that, but with strong fundamentals of basic CSS, at least I can image what it looks like on the inside (or at least enough to realize it probably won’t ever bother me).

One last thing

Yeah… apparently you can’t just learn the basic stuff of HTML, CSS and JS (starting from the most fundamental things to develop strong theoretical foundations) in one sitting a couple of hours before you have to submit your assignment, but if you’re mostly focused in becoming a developer, well… if what you’re trying to do is not relatively (which is probably the case for most people), getting things done is not that hard because some has already made a tool somewhere to address this very problem, so you can just go ahead an implement that!

Also, a little bit intuitively, but that’s pretty much the whole principle behind development, but if instead what you’re trying to do is build things from scratch, then be prepared for scratching your head around the million (almost) useless facts that you’ll have to learn just to make something that most devs out there take for granted.
entry.summary: What did we learn? “HTML is just another MD”. I mean yes… but no… In general, of course HTML is another markup language. I mean, it’s in the name itself.
